title: artemisia vulgaris subsp. tilesii
intro: Artemisia vulgaris subsp. tilesii, also known as Wormwood, is a subspecies of the mugwort plant, characterized by its bitter and astringent properties. The medicinal properties of Artemisia vulgaris subsp. tilesii are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Wormwood include its ability to reduce inflammation, act as an antiseptic, and exhibit antiparasitic properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for digestive issues and malaria prevention. Herbal preparations of Artemisia vulgaris subsp. tilesii include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, from indigestion to fever reduction.
